title: pavonia alnifolia
intro: Pavonia alnifolia is a perennial plant species belonging to the Malvaceae family, commonly known as the rockrose or rosemallow. It is native to Mexico and Central America, and has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The plant's leaves and roots are used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and analgesic properties. Pavonia alnifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote wound healing, reduce inflammation, and alleviate digestive problems.
